attacking_style: Late-race opportunist who attacks in final 500m-2km, whether on climbs, flat finishes, or closing circuits. Shows tactical patience to let others animate before striking with residual power.
team_tactics: Limited domestique support evident (Bahrain TTT weakness at Paris-Nice). Relies on individual positioning and bonus second hunting rather than sustained team tempo control.
terrain_preference: Excels on punchy Ardennes-style climbs (near-win at Fleche Wallonne) and time trials with strong pacing discipline. Can survive chaotic one-day racing and sprint from reduced groups.
vulnerability: Insufficient team support in stage races when needing collective defense. Early time checks in TTs show slower starts despite strong finishing pacing.
key_patterns: Hunts bonus seconds aggressively for GC positioning rather than waiting for stages (Tour de Suisse, Paris-Nice)
Attacks in final 500m-2km window across multiple race types: climbs, flats, circuits (4 instances)
Strong TT finishing power but slow starter; improves exponentially through time trial efforts (Tour de Suisse ITT)
Positions in chase groups rather than initiating, then outsprints survivors (Bretagne Classic, Tour de Suisse)
Tactical maturity beyond age: calculates when to let others work while preserving podium position
form_trajectory: positive: Won Tour de Suisse in June, then took chaotic Bretagne Classic in September showing sustained form and versatility across 2023 season
